channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: America's news TV channel for the Mideast fires its staff and goes off the air after funding cuts
item.description: The head of the U.S.-funded Al Hurra Arabic-language television and online news outlet has terminated most of his staff and TV programming.
item.dc:creator: Sam Magdy And Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China put steep tariffs on U.S. exports. Farmers are worried
item.description: The first Trump administration spent $28 billion bailing out farmers during a trade war with China. The White House has said it's starting to look at how to help this time around.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers reveal whether Americans should pick up the Medicaid tab for illegal immigrants
item.description: Fox News Digital asked lawmakers on Capitol Hill if American taxpayers should have to pick up the tab for Medicaid for illegal immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: RFK Jr. wants to target chronic disease in US tribes. A key program to do that was gutted
item.description: A key program using traditional medicine and foods to tackle chronic disease in Native American and Alaska Native communities has been gutted in the federal health layoffs.
item.dc:creator: Devna Bose, Graham Lee Brewer And Becky Bohrer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump feels in ‘good shape,’ after physical, says he got ‘every question right’ on cognitive test
item.description: President Trump talked to reporters on Friday about his first physical examination and cognitive test of his second term at Walter Reed Medical Center.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge sides with Trump in allowing immigration enforcement in houses of worship
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday sided with the Trump administration in allowing immigration agents to conduct enforcement operations at houses of worship for now, despite a lawsuit filed by religious groups over the new policy. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden's team hid the truth about his health all along: WH press sec
item.description: The Biden administration engaged in a “cover-up" by failing to disclose details about the health of former President Joe Biden, the White House said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump reaches deals with 5 law firms, allowing them to avoid prospect of punishing executive orders
item.description: President Donald Trump has announced deals with five law firms that will allow them to avoid the prospect of a punishing executive order.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: SCOOP: Biden-era grant program described as 'gold bar' scheme by Trump EPA administrator under scrutiny
item.description: House lawmakers are seeking answers on whether a controversial Biden-era green energy grant program was used improperly to boost groups with ties to Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Now that they've passed a budget plan, the hard part begins for Republicans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans narrowly got their budget plan over the finish line. Now comes the hard part. The resolution adopted this week was only a first step that allows Republicans to draft legislation that they can push through Congress without Democratic support.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Department of Health workers rally against Trump administration cuts
item.description: Members of the Minnesota Association of Professional Employees rallied outside a state office building on the grounds of the State Capitol Thursday to speak out against cuts to public health programs.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Trump orders agencies to ‘sunset’ environmental protections
item.description: The order applies to all regulations issued under laws governing things like energy appliance standards, mining and offshore drilling — as well as the Endangered Species Act.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ moves to drop charges against man it labeled MS-13 leader
item.description: The move to dismiss the charges prompted the unusual push from Villatoro Santos’s lawyer seeking to delay the dropping of any charges against his client.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans pass bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
item.description: House Republicans for a second time passed a stand-alone bill that would require proof of citizenship to vote in federal elections and impose voter roll purge requirements on states. The legislation — formally titled the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act — passed in a 220-208 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Where did U.S. humanities grants go? To projects from a baseball film to AI research
item.description: From AI research to historical preservation, programs funded by grants from the National Endowment for the Humanities reach every corner of the U.S. Now the government has terminated those grants.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair, MPR News

item.title: Judge orders White House to allow AP access to news events
item.description: U.S. Judge Trevor N. McFadden rules the White House cannot deny the Associated Press access to news events because the wire service continues to use “Gulf of Mexico” rather than “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service (NPS) has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, WFLA, NBC
